ddls:
CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance log entry */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the machine involved */,
  "shift_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the shift during which the event occurred */,
  "problem_type" TEXT /* Type of problem encountered (e.g., SLANT LEADS, UNEVEN WINDING) */,
  "problem_category" TEXT /* Category of the problem (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical) */,
  "start_down" TEXT /* Timestamp when the machine went down */,
  "start_repair" TEXT /* Timestamp when repair work started */,
  "job_complete" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job was completed */,
  "duration_per_event" REAL /* Duration of the event in hours */,
  "last_downtime" INTEGER /* Number of days since the last downtime event */,
  "repair_personnel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the personnel who performed the repair */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., Preventive, Corrective) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the event */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "alerts_sent" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if alerts were sent for this event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_no" TEXT /* Machine number of the CNC machine */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the CNC machine */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the machine in the facility */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the machine was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the machine (e.g., Operational, Down) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shifts" (
  "shift_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shift */,
  "shift_name" TEXT /* Name of the shift (e.g., DAY, NIGHT) */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the shift */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the shift */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shift_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for user login */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for user login */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Maintenance Personnel, Supervisor, Administrator) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_schedule" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance schedule entry */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the machine */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., Preventive, Corrective) */,
  "scheduled_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance is scheduled */,
  "completed" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the maintenance is completed */,
  "completed_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was completed */,
  "assigned_personnel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the personnel assigned to the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "repair_personnel" (
  "personnel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance personnel */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the personnel */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position of the personnel (e.g., Technician, Engineer) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the personnel */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the personnel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("personnel_id")
)
